market_summary: US equity futures are pointing to a lower open, with the Nasdaq underperforming as a sharp selloff in SK Hynix (down 15% in Seoul) spills over into the broader semiconductor complex. The S&P 500 and Dow are also under pressure as oil prices surge above $80/bbl following renewed US-Iran airstrikes and President Trump's proposed 20% toll on Strait of Hormuz cargo. Defensive sectors like healthcare and energy are attracting bids, while tech and telecom face headwinds from AI trade angst and a Starlink threat to telcos. The market is bracing for a pivotal week of earnings, with JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, and other big banks reporting Tuesday.
macro_summary: Geopolitical Shock and Rotation The market is repricing geopolitical risk, but the underlying earnings and growth story remains intact. The key is whether this is a buying opportunity or the start of a deeper correction. The market is in a 'risk-off but not panic' regime. The US-Iran escalation and SK Hynix rout are forcing a rotation out of high-beta tech and into energy, healthcare, and value. The move is orderly, but the VIX spike and negative breadth suggest that dip-buyers are hesitant. The market is waiting for the next catalyst, which will likely come from bank earnings or a de-escalation in the Middle East. The macro backdrop is dominated by a sudden escalation in geopolitical risk, which is complicating the inflation and rate outlook. Oil's surge above $80/bbl adds upside risk to inflation prints, potentially delaying Fed rate cuts. However, the market is still pricing in a high probability of a cut in September. The key tension is between a 'bad news is good news' regime (where weaker data supports cuts) and a 'stagflation' scenario (where higher oil and slower growth coexist).
risk_summary: Risk appetite is clearly deteriorating. The VIX is spiking above 18, and put/call ratios are rising. Breadth is negative in pre-market, with decliners outpacing advancers 3:1 on the NYSE. The market is pricing in a higher probability of a 'hard landing' or 'stagflation' scenario, but the move is still orderly, suggesting no panic. The key risk is that the SK Hynix rout and geopolitical headlines trigger a cascade of stop-losses and forced selling in the first hour of trading.

top_events.title: US and Iran exchange airstrikes; Trump proposes 20% toll on Strait of Hormuz cargo
top_events.summary: The US and Iran traded missile and drone airstrikes over the weekend. President Trump then proposed a 20% toll on cargo ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz and reimposed a naval blockade. Oil prices surged above $80/bbl.

top_events.title: SK Hynix shares plunge 15% in Seoul, worst day in 18 years
top_events.summary: SK Hynix's stock had its worst single-day decline in 18 years on the Korean exchange, falling 15%. The selloff was driven by a combination of geopolitical risk, profit-taking, and concerns about AI demand. The move is spilling over into US chip stocks.

top_events.title: Big banks poised to report booming Q2 revenue; JPMorgan, Goldman, BofA report Tuesday
top_events.summary: Major US banks are set to report strong Q2 results, driven by the SpaceX IPO, Iran war volatility, and a rebound in commercial lending. This is the most anticipated earnings week for the financial sector.

top_events.title: S&P 500 performs best when Fed keeps rates steady: HB Wealth (SPY:NYSEARCA)
top_events.summary: HB Wealth reports that the S&P 500 performs best when the Federal Reserve keeps interest rates steady.

top_events.title: Trump proposes 20% toll on cargo through Strait of Hormuz; restarts Iran blockade
top_events.summary: President Trump proposes a 20% toll on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz and restarts the naval blockade on Iran.

top_events.title: Global oil prices top $80 a barrel after Trump reimposes Strait of Hormuz blockade and charges 20% fee for cargo shipped through
top_events.summary: Oil prices surge above $80 per barrel following President Trump's reimposition of the Strait of Hormuz blockade and a 20% cargo fee.

top_events.title: Oil prices rise following the latest fighting in the Middle East, as AI stocks sink
top_events.summary: Oil prices increase due to renewed Middle East conflict, while AI stocks decline.

top_events.title: Apollo’s Slok Warns of Dollar’s Vulnerability to an AI Pullback
top_events.summary: Apollo's chief economist Torsten Slok warns that the US dollar is vulnerable to a pullback in AI-related investments.

sector_summary: Positive sectors/themes: Energy (XLE), Healthcare (XLV), Defense (ITA), Gold Miners (GDX); Negative sectors/themes: Semiconductors (SMH), Telecom (IYZ), Airlines (JETS), Consumer Discretionary (XLY); Themes to watch: Geopolitical risk premium, AI trade rotation, Value vs. Growth, Earnings season kickoff
